title: sambucus racemosa f. rosiflora
intro: Sambucus racemosa f. rosiflora, also known as the Rose Elder or Red Elderberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Adoxaceae family. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its rich content of flavonoids, anthocyanins, and other bioactive compounds, which possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Sambucus racemosa f. rosiflora have been associated with various health benefits, including the reduction of fever, relief of respiratory issues such as colds and flu, and the support of immune function. Herbal preparations of Sambucus racemosa f. rosiflora, such as tinctures, infusions, and syrups, can be made from its flowers, berries, or leaves, and are often used to promote overall well-being and prevent illness.
